DDR5-6400.reason: NEMIX $1,488 25-day anchor — longest in tracking history, entering 7th work week. DDR5 spot 4-phase pattern structurally confirmed at $41.500 all-time high. Samsung union vote high turnout (80%+) but outcome uncertain through May 27 — short-term supply unchanged. Corsair/CXMT supply diversification is earliest normalization roadmap but impact 14-18 months out. DRAM Q1 +110-115% + Micron Crucial exit + Kingston SKU EOL + full chain sold out + AI demand structural.

DDR5-5600.reason: DDR5 spot $41.500 all-time high with 4-phase 'dip-bounce-accelerate-sustain' pattern confirmed. $41 line completed 3-tier evolution: resistance→support→fear-of-missing-out line→new baseline floor. 12-day cumulative +4.19%. NEMIX 96GB $3,200 23-day persistence — recommend formal tracking column. Corsair/CXMT signal is consumer-DDR5 focused with near-zero enterprise RDIMM impact in 2026.

PCIe4 TLC.reason: 20-day brand split 3.4-5.5x structural Day 20 (4th week). Goldman Sachs NAND 4.2% global deficit (largest since 2011) + NAND Q2 +70-75% + 6500 ION EOL Day 17 + MLC NAND tripled + full chain sold out. Tracking lag 62-158% — actual procurement cost ¥29,136-¥46,571 vs tracking ¥18,094 (1.6-2.6x). DiscTech offline Day 25 entering 5th week.

PCIe5 TLC.reason: 30.72TB SS $17,700 Day 17 sustained price increase (entering 4th week) + 8-ref 4-brand Day 25. ⚠️ PM9D3a 'currently not deliverable' (Compuram) but partial sources suggest June 1 delivery possible — mixed signals. Micron 9550 Pro 5-ref cluster ($16,939-$19,573) remains most solid. SHI $19,573 re-confirmed today. Brand 3-tier Day 13. Samsung 7.68TB band $2,499-$3,580 maintained. Price increase entering 4th week — 'competition-suppresses-price' window substantively closing.

PCIe4 QLC.reason: ⚡ Mouser 61.44TB U.2 Day 5 only 1 unit — DEEP CONFIRMATION+, full work week + weekend with zero replenishment. 122.88TB E1.L only 2 units — large-capacity QLC across-the-board severe shortage. Goldman Sachs NAND 4.2% deficit. 6500 ION EOL (2026/8/25) → 30.72TB QLC dual→single source. 5 days without replenishment excludes all temporary explanations — channel inventory has structurally depleted. If large-capacity QLC SSD is actually needed, every day the supply window is irreversibly closing.

escalatedRisks.status: NEW — Day 1. First verified Western brand integrating Chinese DRAM.
escalatedRisks.detail: Corsair integrating CXMT DDR5 chips due to traditional supplier (Samsung/SK Hynix/Micron) undersupply. CXMT DRAM ~$150 vs global $300-400. China 'Epic Expansion' nearly doubling wafer output. Earliest price normalization roadmap — but impact 14-18 months out (H2 2027-2028).

escalatedRisks.status: NEW — Day 1. Major suppliers fully booked.
escalatedRisks.detail: Micron CEO Sanjay Mehrotra reiterates tight DRAM/NAND supply through 2027 and beyond. Major memory suppliers already fully booked. Confirms structural undersupply independent of Samsung union situation.
